trigger: Round 02 closeout accepted with a finding that the old Round 03 contract overlaps already completed adaptation and admission work.
historicalPlannedGoal: Adapt approved candidates into neutral, portable, non-duplicative Skill payloads or route them into existing Skills, recipes, references, or rejections.
proposedGoal: Run a bounded, evidence-backed capability survey that starts from human-AI collaboration demand coordinates, measures native and runtime coverage, discovers and clusters public candidate metadata, compares native, single-Skill, composed-Skill, and non-Skill Harness alternatives, and proves any residual gap before later adoption, adaptation, repository authoring, release, or standards extraction.

allowed: dated native, official, runtime, installed, and already-authorized capability baseline design
read-only public metadata discovery after this rebaseline is accepted
capability clustering, deduplication, and marginal-yield stopping evidence
small representative source-pinned deep reviews without candidate execution
STM-01 through STM-26, P1 through P24, and SG-01 through SG-12 coverage mapping
native, single-Skill, composed-Skill, Recipe, policy, schema, test, Hook, CI, human-review, and project-standard alternative comparison
post-gap carrier comparison across Skill-only, Skill-plus-Hook, Hook or other non-Skill Harness, project standard, and human-authority paths
stage result package and next-round candidate preparation
blocked: installing, enabling, connecting, or executing candidate Skills or third-party code
account connection, OAuth, credential exposure, payment, or private-data expansion
live Codex, agents, cc-switch, Claude, TRAE, WorkBuddy, or other runtime mutation
Skill admission, release-manifest mutation, or repository-authored gap-fill implementation
claiming universal cross-Agent coverage or global program completion

dataAndAuthorityBoundary.publicExternalData: Read-only GitHub repository metadata, trees, licenses, README files, SKILL.md files, and only the references needed for bounded classification or representative deep review.
dataAndAuthorityBoundary.localInputs: Current repository truth plus registry/round03-demand-coordinate-source-contract.json, which pins explicitly bound read-only demand-coordinate evidence; consumer repositories remain non-authoritative inputs and are not write targets.
dataAndAuthorityBoundary.executionBoundary: No candidate code, scripts, hooks, dependencies, commands, or network-capable payloads may be executed.
dataAndAuthorityBoundary.accountBoundary: No new account, OAuth, credential, paid service, private repository, or broader data-domain access.
dataAndAuthorityBoundary.writeBoundary: Writes are limited to reviewed artifacts in agent-skills-curated; no live runtime, user configuration, CALIBRATION, ASSETS, or MERIDIAN write is authorized.
dataAndAuthorityBoundary.releaseBoundary: Survey evidence cannot directly admit payload, mutate the release manifest, promote a hard standard, or authorize installation.

safeParallelism: native and runtime baselines may run in parallel by explicitly bounded host, model, loader, permission, and workspace context
metadata triage may run in parallel by independent capability cluster after demand and data boundaries are bound
source-pinned representative reviews may run in parallel by independent cluster and must join before alternative comparison
executionSemantics.stopRule: Stop the bounded discovery pass when additional candidates predominantly map to existing clusters and no longer materially change coverage, architecture alternatives, candidate dispositions, or residual-gap evidence; record remaining uncertainty and recheck triggers.
rerouteTriggers: host, model, loader, permission, or capability-health change
license, provenance, security, portability, or maintenance concern
native or composed alternative covers the need
candidate conflict, hidden dependency, or verification failure
marginal discovery yield falls below decision-changing value

gapFillCarrierDecision.entryCondition: A material residual gap is supported by evidence after native, official/runtime, existing Skill, composed Skill, and other non-Skill alternatives are compared.
options: reuse or improve an existing native, runtime, official, curated, or third-party path
repository-authored Skill with a consumer-owned Hook
Hook or another non-Skill Harness without a new Skill
project-owned hard standard or deterministic control
hold because the gap remains unproven
hookSuitabilitySignals: event-driven recall is materially unreliable without a trigger
a bounded pre-action or post-action check is required
the Hook can remain thin, inspectable, reversible, and consumer-owned
the Hook materially reduces failure without excessive noise, latency, permission, or maintenance cost
hookRejectionSignals: the Skill or project standard is sufficient without event automation
the Hook would become an authority source or universal controller
the host lacks a portable, testable, and disableable Hook surface
the Hook requires hidden mutation, broader data access, credentials, or unbounded side effects
false positives, context injection, startup cost, or maintenance rent exceed the measured benefit
gapFillCarrierDecision.authorityRule: A Hook is an optional recall or bounded enforcement aid, never evidence, approval, installation authority, release authority, or permission to bypass the consuming project, runtime, user, or human decision boundary.
gapFillCarrierDecision.default: no Hook unless the supported residual gap and host-specific verification justify it

requiredResultPackage: dated native Agent and runtime capability baseline
clustered and deduplicated candidate Skills inventory
STM, P, and SG capability coverage matrix
single-Skill and composed Skill or non-Skill Harness alternatives
host, model, reasoning, loader, permission, workspace, and evidence limitations
intent-contract, capability-router, closure-contract, and conditional Skill-only, Skill-plus-Hook, non-Skill Hook, or no-Hook recommendations
discovery stop rationale, recheck triggers, upstream-update implications, and next-round candidates
verificationSurface: every result-package item maps to acceptance, verification, and evidence identifiers
candidate records preserve immutable source identity, license, dependencies, authority, portability, overlap, cost, lifecycle, and claim limits
coverage claims distinguish metadata, structural, behavioral, host-specific, cross-Agent, and longitudinal evidence
composition analysis records ordering, handoff, shared state, conflicts, failure propagation, and total context and maintenance cost
repository verifier, topology check, manifest check, routing simulation, tests, and diff check pass
no blocked side effect appears in the Git diff or external state
nextGate: bounded public metadata discovery for EL-01, EL-02, and EL-04
